mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP indicates expanding aggregate demand, which drives corporate revenue and earnings higher, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward correlation between GDPC1 year-over-year growth and SPX forward 60-day returns is non-positive over any 10-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions, reducing consumer spending and corporate profits, which depresses equity prices over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.2pp month-over-month
failure_condition: Average 20-day forward return following UNRATE trigger events is non-negative or not statistically different from zero at 95% confidence.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated short-term interest rates combined with very low unemployment simultaneously compress equity valuations through higher discount rates and signal an overheated labor market that prompts further tightening, leading to negative forward returns.
failure_condition: In instances where FEDFUNDS > 5% and UNRATE < 4% simultaneously, the mean 20-day forward return is positive or not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: During early stages of a manufacturing expansion, rising industrial production signals improving capacity utilization and supply-side strength that boosts corporate profits and stock prices over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: When INDPRO growth > 0.5%, the subsequent 20-day forward returns are not higher than the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth indicates robust consumer demand, which directly drives near-term earnings expectations and supports higher equity prices over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: The 5-day forward return correlation with RSAFS month-over-month growth is non-positive at a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the federal funds rate of 25 basis points or more within one month signals aggressive monetary tightening that raises the cost of capital and lowers equity valuations, producing negative forward returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by at least 25bp month-over-month
failure_condition: Average 20-day return following FEDFUNDS jump events is non-negative or zero.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts are rising and the federal funds rate is declining, it signals an accommodative housing market that stimulates construction, employment, and consumer wealth, leading to positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: When HOUST rises and FEDFUNDS falls simultaneously, the mean 60-day forward return is not positive or fails to exceed the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: In the early expansion phase, strong payroll growth of more than 150k per month reinforces the recovery narrative and investor confidence, pushing stock prices higher over a one-week horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change exceeds 150 (thousands of persons)
failure_condition: In instances with PAYEMS growth > 150k, the 5-day forward return is not systematically higher than the unconditional median at a 1% significance level.
